event_date_text: Events and meetings organized by UNDRR UNDRR complements Member States’ efforts to implement the Sendai Framework through its continued support for an all-of-society multi-stakeholder approach. More specifically, UNDRR works with Member States to develop and implement their DRR strategies and DRR-related aspects of the UN Sustainable Development Cooperation Frameworks and Common Country Analyses. event_date_text: Upcoming Events Past Events Upcoming Events 6 July 2026 - 15 July 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) The High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) will be convened from Monday, 6 July, to Wednesday, 15 July 2026, under the auspices of the Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC). 11 July 2026 UN Day Global Event Human Rights World Population Day World Population Day is celebrated annually on 11 July to focus attention on the urgency and importance of population issues. 9 August 2026 UN Day Global Event Human Rights International Day of the World's Indigenous Peoples In order to raise awareness of the needs of these population groups, every 9 August commemorates the International Day of the World’s Indigenous Peoples, chosen in recognition of the first meeting of the UN Working Group on Indigenous Populations held in Geneva in 1982. 12 August 2026 UN Day Global Event Youth International Youth Day International Youth Day is commemorated every year on 12 August, bringing youth issues to the attention of the international community and celebrating the potential of youth as partners in today’s global society. The idea for International Youth Day was proposed in 1991 by the young people who were gathered in Vienna, Austria, for the first session of the World Youth Forum of the United Nations System. 19 August 2026 UN Day Global Event Human Rights World Humanitarian Day World Humanitarian Day is commemorated every year on 19 August to pay tribute to humanitarian workers killed and injured in the course of their work, and to honor all aid and health workers who continue to provide life-saving support and protection to people most in need. The Day highlights the importance, effectiveness, and positive impact of humanitarian work. 8 September 2026 - 22 September 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs 81st session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA 81) The 81st session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA 81) will open on Tuesday, 8 September 2026. The first day of the high-level General Debate will be Tuesday, 22 September 2026. 20 September 2026 UN-Habitat Event Global Event Climate Change World Cleanup Day On 8 December 2023, the United Nations General Assembly, in its seventy-eighth session, unanimously adopted resolution 78/122 “World Cleanup Day”, which proclaims 20 September as World Cleanup Day. The resolution invites all Member States, organizations of the United Nations system, other international and regional organizations, and other relevant stakeholders – including civil society, the private sector and academia – to observe… 5 October 2026 UN Day Global Event Localizing the SDGs World Habitat Day World Habitat Day is marked on the first Monday of October each year and is recognized by the United Nations to reflect on the state of towns and cities, and on the basic right of all to adequate shelter. The day is also intended to remind the world that everyone has the power and the responsibility to shape the future of towns and cities. 5 October 2026 - 6 October 2026 Conference Physical Event Innovation Smart City Expo Miami Among its most visible contributions is the internationally recognized Smart City Expo Miami, a flagship event that convenes global leaders, policymakers, business executives, and civic innovators each year. The expo has evolved into the leading platform for smart city dialogue and collaboration in the United States, connecting South Florida's unique local energy with global intelligence and insight. By promoting cutting-edge ideas in… 6 October 2026 Partner Event Global Event Housing World Architecture Day Created by the UIA in 1985, World Architecture Day is celebrated annually on the first Monday of October coinciding with the UN-Habitat Global Observance of the World Habitat Day. 13 October 2026 UN Day Global Event Resilience and Risk Reduction International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction The International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction was started in 1989, after a call by the United Nations General Assembly for a day to promote a global culture of risk-awareness and disaster reduction. Held every 13 October, the day celebrates how people and communities around the world are reducing their exposure to disasters and raising awareness about the importance of reining in the risks that they face. 19 October 2026 - 30 October 2026 Conference Physical Event Climate Change UN Biodiversity Conference (CBD COP 17) The 17th meeting of the Conference of the Parties (COP) to the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) will convene in October 2026. 31 October 2026 UN-Habitat Event Global Event Localizing the SDGs World Cities Day World Cities Day brings Urban October to an end on 31 October each year and was first celebrated in 2014. As with World Habitat Day, a global observance is held in a different city each year and the day focuses on a specific theme. 2 December 2026 - 4 December 2026 Conference Physical Event Water and Sanitation United Nations Water Conference Achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 6 on Clean Water and Sanitation is an essential end in itself and is also critical to progress on the 2030 Agenda as a whole. As we enter the final 5 years of the 2030 Agenda and its SDGs, the world must surge investment, innovation, political will and a spirit of solidarity into SDG 6. The 2026 United Nations Water Conference, co-hosted by the United Arab Emirates and Senegal, and convened… 3 December 2026 UN Day Global Event Disability International Day of Persons with Disabilities International Day of Persons with Disabilities (IDPD) is a UN day that is celebrated every year on 3 December. The day is about promoting the rights and well-being of persons with disabilities at every level of society and development, and to raise awareness of the situation of persons with disabilities in all aspects of political, social, economic, and cultural life. By addressing today’s most critical sustainability challenges, it aims to help re-envision a multilateralism that is fit for today’s world. Established by the UN General Assembly in 1972, the Day inspires and is celebrated by millions of people across the world, who participate in online and in-person activities and events around the world aimed at accelerating environmental progress for people and planet. 22 May 2026 UN Day Global Event Climate Change International Day for Biological Diversity As the global community is called to re-examine our relationship to the natural world, one thing is certain: despite all our technological advances we are completely dependent on healthy and vibrant ecosystems for our water, food, medicines, clothes, fuel, shelter and energy. 17 May 2026 - 22 May 2026 Conference Physical Event New Urban Agenda World Urban Forum (WUF13) WUF was established in 2001 by the United Nations to examine one of the most pressing issues facing the world today: rapid urbanization and its impact on communities, cities, economies, climate change and policies. The first WUF was held in Nairobi, Kenya in 2002 and has been held around the world ever since. Representatives of national, regional and local governments, academics, business people, community leaders, urban planners and… 28 April 2026 - 30 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Twelfth Session of the Africa Regional Forum on Sustainable Development The Twelfth Session of the Africa Regional Forum on Sustainable Development is scheduled to take place from 28 to 30 April 2026. This session will focus on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and will be a platform for assessing progress and exchanging knowledge, best practices, and policy solutions to support the implementation of the 2030 Agenda. 22 April 2026 UN Day Global Event Climate Change International Mother Earth Day International Mother Earth Day aims to raise global public awareness of the challenges to the well-being of the planet and all the life it supports. The Day also recognizes a collective responsibility, as called for in the 1992 Rio Declaration, to promote harmony with nature and the Earth, to achieve a just balance among the economic, social, and environmental needs of the present and future generations of humanity. 21 April 2026 - 23 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Arab Forum for Sustainable Development The Arab Forum for Sustainable Development (AFSD) is the primary regional mechanism for the follow-up and review of the implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development in the Arab region. AFSD brings together Arab Governments and a wide range of stakeholders to discuss progress, review national and regional experiences, and consider the latest developments and implementation mechanisms of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable… 21 April 2026 - 22 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs UNECE Regional Forum on Sustainable Development The Regional Forum on Sustainable Development was established by the Economic Commission for Europe at its sixty-seventh session in April 2017, with the aim to create “a regional mechanism to follow-up and review the implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development” 14 April 2026 - 16 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Youth Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) Youth Forum The Forum provides a platform for young people to engage in a dialogue with Member States and other actors to voice their views, concerns, and galvanize actions on how to transform the world into a fairer, greener, and more sustainable place guided by the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). 13 April 2026 - 16 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Advocacy Forum of the Countries of Latin America and the Caribbean on Sustainable Development The Forum of the Countries of Latin America and the Caribbean on Sustainable Development will be State-led and open to the participation of Latin American and Caribbean countries. It will be convened under the auspices of ECLAC and will be guided by the principles established for all follow-up and review processes by the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted in September 2015 by the United Nations General Assembly. It will involve… 8 April 2026 - 10 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Housing Second Africa Urban Forum (AUF2) The Second Africa Urban Forum (AUF2), convened by the African Union Commission and hosted by the Government of Kenya with technical support from UN-Habitat and UNECA, will be held in Nairobi, Kenya, from 8 to 10 April under the theme “Adequate Housing for All: Advancing Socio-economic and Environmental Transformation towards the realization of Agenda 2063.” Held ahead of the… 30 March 2026 Partner Event Global Event Climate Change International Day of Zero Waste The International Day of Zero Waste, observed annually on 30 March, highlights both the importance of bolstering waste management globally and the need to promote sustainable consumption and production patterns. 25 March 2026 - 27 March 2026 Conference Physical Event Climate Change World Sustainable Development Summit The World Sustainable Development Summit (WSDS) is the annual flagship multistakeholder convening organized by The Energy and Resources Institute (TERI). Instituted in 2001, the Summit series has a legacy of over two decades in making ‘sustainable development’ a globally shared goal. Over the years, the Summit platform has brought together thought leaders, heads of state and government, scholars, corporates, youth groups, and civil society… 22 March 2026 UN Day Global Event Water and Sanitation World Water Day World Water Day celebrates water and inspires action to tackle the global water crisis. event_date_text: Shutterstock Shutterstock Calendar Get the latest on UNESCO activities worldwide Home All events Statutory meetings Informal meetings Events DG's schedule Other events Jump to : Next international days Calls for applications and statutory deadlines This Month at UNESCO Tech Diplomacy Global Forum 2026 2 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 10 am-5pm, Room I In an increasingly fragmented global landscape, the Forum brings together world leaders, policymakers, technologists, and civil society to forge new pathways for international cooperation. It will address the critical challenges of building trust across borders, sectors, and communities in the digital age. Spotlight Session “The Value of Journalism: Contributions to Security, Stability and Sustainable Development” 2 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 4.30-6 pm, Room IV UNESCO promotes press freedom and the safety of journalists as fundamental pillars of freedom of expression and access to information. Across the world—including in Sudan, where journalists continue to face bullets, exile, and fear—UNESCO works to protect media professionals from violence, support safe and enabling environments for independent reporting, and strengthen efforts to end impunity for crimes committed against journalists. International Day for African and Afro-descendant Women 3 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 10 am-6 pm, Room II The Permanent Delegation of Mali to UNESCO will host an event on 3 July to mark the Day. Observed annually on 25 July, the International Day honours the invaluable contributions of African and Afro-descendant women worldwide. It recognizes their vital role in the social, economic, cultural, and political development of their communities while promoting gender equality, empowerment, and the full participation and visibility of their voices. Global Dialogue on AI Governance 6-7 July 2026 (Palexpo Geneva, Switzerland) The first session of the Global Dialogue will bring together governments, international organizations, academia, the private sector, and civil society for two days of high-level discussions on the future of artificial intelligence governance. The event will feature a high-level segment, thematic sessions, and side events that foster inclusive dialogue and international cooperation on responsible AI development and use. WSIS Forum 2026 6-10 July 2026 (Geneva, Switzerland) The World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS) serves as the central multistakeholder convening platform mandated to advance the implementation of the WSIS Action Lines and support global digital cooperation. Anchored in the renewed UNGA mandate, it brings together governments, the private sector, civil society, technical communities, academia, youth, and international organizations in a uniquely inclusive environment for dialogue, collaboration, and action. AI for Good Global Summit 7-10 July 2026 (Geneva, Switzerland) The Summit is the United Nations’ leading event on artificial intelligence, organized by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with numerous UN agencies, including UNESCO. It brings together governments, researchers, industry leaders, civil society, and innovators to showcase practical AI solutions that support the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Large Marine Ecosystems Consultation Meeting (LME25) 7-9 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 9.30 am-5.30 pm, Room IX The Annual Consultation Meeting on Large Marine Ecosystems (LMEs) and Coastal Partners is instrumental in fostering and enhancing cooperation within the LME community and beyond. It serves as an interactive platform for sharing expertise and holding strategic discussions, engaging primarily the Global Environment Facility (GEF)-funded marine portfolio and a wide range of stakeholders, including representatives from Regional Seas bodies, Fisheries Management Organizations, international NGOs, and partner organizations. Spotlight Session on South Sudan – Safeguarding People and Heritage in Crisis in South Sudan 9 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France),10-11.30 am, Room XI This UNESCO-hosted spotlight session brings together stakeholders to discuss the challenges of empowering people and safeguarding cultural heritage during crises in South Sudan. The session highlights the importance of protecting cultural heritage alongside humanitarian efforts, while fostering dialogue and collaboration to strengthen resilience and support sustainable recovery. Transforming Education Summit +4: System Transformation and Resilience for SDG 4 + High-level Steering Committee Leaders Meeting on SDG4 10 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 9 am-6 pm, Rooms I, II, IX, X, XI TES+4 brings together leaders and partners to review progress since the 2022 UN Transforming Education Summit and accelerate action towards achieving SDG 4. The event will focus on education system transformation and resilience, while the High-level Steering Committee Leaders Meeting on SDG 4 will support continued collaboration towards quality education for all. World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 13-21 July 2026 (Busan, South Korea) The Forum brings together young professionals from around the world to engage in dialogue, learning and exchange on the role of communities, youth and education in strengthening the protection, management and transmission of World Heritage. At the end of the Forum, they will collectively present their Declaration at the 48th session of the World Heritage Committee. 2026 Global Conference on the International Decade of Sciences for Sustainable Development (IDSSD 2024-2033) 15-17 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), Rooms I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII, IX, XI UNESCO welcomes you to join the first Global Conference of the International Decade of Sciences for Sustainable Development (IDSSD), a hybrid event that emphasizes the strengthening of the role of science, innovation, and international cooperation in advancing sustainable development and accelerating progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals. An exhibition on UNESCO Fences will also be presented from 15 July to 31 August 2026. 48th session of the World Heritage Committee 19-29 July 2026 (Busan, South Korea) The session will bring together representatives from 21 Member States, elected from among the 196 States Parties to the World Heritage Convention. This Committe will examine nominations for the inscription of new sites and the state of conservation of sites already inscribed on the List, and discuss key issues related to the protection of the world’s cultural and natural heritage. Wikimedia annual conference 21-25 July 2026 (Paris, France) Wikimania 2026, the 21st annual Wikimedia conference, will bring together the global Wikimedia community in person in Paris and online. The conference will also celebrate the 25th anniversary of the pioneer Wikipedias, under the theme "Freedom, Equity, Reliability," highlighting the movement's commitment to protecting free, reliable, and inclusive access to knowledge. conference_title: Spotlight Session “The Value of Journalism: Contributions to Security, Stability and Sustainable Development”
event_date_text: Spotlight Session “The Value of Journalism: Contributions to Security, Stability and Sustainable Development” 2 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 4.30-6 pm, Room IV UNESCO promotes press freedom and the safety of journalists as fundamental pillars of freedom of expression and access to information. Across the world—including in Sudan, where journalists continue to face bullets, exile, and fear—UNESCO works to protect media professionals from violence, support safe and enabling environments for independent reporting, and strengthen efforts to end impunity for crimes committed against journalists.

conference_title: International Day for African and Afro-descendant Women
event_date_text: International Day for African and Afro-descendant Women 3 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 10 am-6 pm, Room II The Permanent Delegation of Mali to UNESCO will host an event on 3 July to mark the Day. Observed annually on 25 July, the International Day honours the invaluable contributions of African and Afro-descendant women worldwide. It recognizes their vital role in the social, economic, cultural, and political development of their communities while promoting gender equality, empowerment, and the full participation and visibility of their voices. Global Dialogue on AI Governance 6-7 July 2026 (Palexpo Geneva, Switzerland) The first session of the Global Dialogue will bring together governments, international organizations, academia, the private sector, and civil society for two days of high-level discussions on the future of artificial intelligence governance. The event will feature a high-level segment, thematic sessions, and side events that foster inclusive dialogue and international cooperation on responsible AI development and use. WSIS Forum 2026 6-10 July 2026 (Geneva, Switzerland) The World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS) serves as the central multistakeholder convening platform mandated to advance the implementation of the WSIS Action Lines and support global digital cooperation. Anchored in the renewed UNGA mandate, it brings together governments, the private sector, civil society, technical communities, academia, youth, and international organizations in a uniquely inclusive environment for dialogue, collaboration, and action. AI for Good Global Summit 7-10 July 2026 (Geneva, Switzerland) The Summit is the United Nations’ leading event on artificial intelligence, organized by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with numerous UN agencies, including UNESCO. It brings together governments, researchers, industry leaders, civil society, and innovators to showcase practical AI solutions that support the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Large Marine Ecosystems Consultation Meeting (LME25) 7-9 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 9.30 am-5.30 pm, Room IX The Annual Consultation Meeting on Large Marine Ecosystems (LMEs) and Coastal Partners is instrumental in fostering and enhancing cooperation within the LME community and beyond. It serves as an interactive platform for sharing expertise and holding strategic discussions, engaging primarily the Global Environment Facility (GEF)-funded marine portfolio and a wide range of stakeholders, including representatives from Regional Seas bodies, Fisheries Management Organizations, international NGOs, and partner organizations. Spotlight Session on South Sudan – Safeguarding People and Heritage in Crisis in South Sudan 9 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France),10-11.30 am, Room XI This UNESCO-hosted spotlight session brings together stakeholders to discuss the challenges of empowering people and safeguarding cultural heritage during crises in South Sudan. The session highlights the importance of protecting cultural heritage alongside humanitarian efforts, while fostering dialogue and collaboration to strengthen resilience and support sustainable recovery. Transforming Education Summit +4: System Transformation and Resilience for SDG 4 + High-level Steering Committee Leaders Meeting on SDG4 10 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), 9 am-6 pm, Rooms I, II, IX, X, XI TES+4 brings together leaders and partners to review progress since the 2022 UN Transforming Education Summit and accelerate action towards achieving SDG 4. The event will focus on education system transformation and resilience, while the High-level Steering Committee Leaders Meeting on SDG 4 will support continued collaboration towards quality education for all.

event_date_text: World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 13-21 July 2026 (Busan, South Korea) The Forum brings together young professionals from around the world to engage in dialogue, learning and exchange on the role of communities, youth and education in strengthening the protection, management and transmission of World Heritage. At the end of the Forum, they will collectively present their Declaration at the 48th session of the World Heritage Committee. 2026 Global Conference on the International Decade of Sciences for Sustainable Development (IDSSD 2024-2033) 15-17 July 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France), Rooms I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII, IX, XI UNESCO welcomes you to join the first Global Conference of the International Decade of Sciences for Sustainable Development (IDSSD), a hybrid event that emphasizes the strengthening of the role of science, innovation, and international cooperation in advancing sustainable development and accelerating progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals. An exhibition on UNESCO Fences will also be presented from 15 July to 31 August 2026. 48th session of the World Heritage Committee 19-29 July 2026 (Busan, South Korea) The session will bring together representatives from 21 Member States, elected from among the 196 States Parties to the World Heritage Convention. This Committe will examine nominations for the inscription of new sites and the state of conservation of sites already inscribed on the List, and discuss key issues related to the protection of the world’s cultural and natural heritage. Wikimedia annual conference 21-25 July 2026 (Paris, France) Wikimania 2026, the 21st annual Wikimedia conference, will bring together the global Wikimedia community in person in Paris and online. The conference will also celebrate the 25th anniversary of the pioneer Wikipedias, under the theme "Freedom, Equity, Reliability," highlighting the movement's commitment to protecting free, reliable, and inclusive access to knowledge.

event_date_text: UNESCO UNESCO General Conference The General Conference is the highest governing body of UNESCO. Home President 43rd Session Documents Previous Sessions Secretariat The General Conference determines the policies and the main lines of work of the Organization Its duty is to set the programmes and the budget of UNESCO. It also elects the Members of the Executive Board and appoints, every four years, the Director-General. The working languages of the General Conference are Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian and Spanish. The General Conference consists of the representatives of UNESCO's Member States. It meets every two years, and is attended by Member States and Associate Members, together with observers for non-Member States, intergovernmental organizations and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). Each country has one vote, irrespective of its size or the extent of its contribution to the budget.
